\newline

\newline
\\

\newline

\newline

(d) Flo makes 84 cakes.
\newline
\\
She sells 35 of these cakes.
\newline

Calculate the percentage of the cakes that she sells.
\newline

\newline
\\

\newline

 The cost to make each dessert increases to \$13.60.

(b)

\newline
\\
Bag $B$ contains 5 black marbles and 8 white marbles.
\newline

(i)  Write down the probability that a marble picked at random from bag $A$ is black.
\newline

 Toby says,

\begin{quote}
`You are more likely to pick a black marble at random from bag $B$ than from bag $A$ because bag $B$ has more black marbles.
\newline
'
\end{quote}

Is Toby correct?\\
Give a reason for your answer.
\newline

 because 

 Toby adds some marbles to bag $B$.
\newline
\\
The probability of picking a black marble at random from either bag is now the same.
\newline

tex:
The scale drawing shows the position of town $R$ on a map.
\newline
\\
The scale is 1 centimetre represents 5 kilometres.
\newline

(a) Town $M$ is $36\,\text{km}$ from $R$ on a bearing of 163°.
\newline

Mark the position of $M$ on the map.
\newline

(b) A railway track, $36\,\text{km}$ long, is to be built in a straight line from $R$ to $M$.
\newline

(i)  The track costs \$1070 per metre to build.
\newline

Work out the cost of building the track.
\newline

 15 people can build 60 metres of track per day.
\newline

Work out how many days it will take 45 people to build the whole track.
\newline

(c) Trains will travel the $36\,\text{km}$ at an average speed of $75\,\text{km/h}$.
\newline

Work out the journey time.
\newline
\\
Give your answer in minutes.
\newline

(d) Town $K$ is on a bearing of 312° from $R$.
\newline

Work out the bearing of $R$ from $K$.
\newline

qtype: interpreting graphs of reciprocal functions and substitution
tex:
The diagram shows the graph of $y = \dfrac{k}{x}$ for $1 \leqslant x \leqslant 8$.
\newline

(a) Use the graph to find the value of $x$ when $y = 4$.
\newline

(b) (i)  Show that $k = 8$.
\newline

 Calculate the value of $y$ when $x = 250$.
\newline

(c) (i)  Complete this table of values for $y = \dfrac{8}{x}$.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
$x$ & $-8$ & $-4$ & $-2$ & $-1$ \\
\hline
$y$ & & & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

 On the grid, draw the graph of $y = \dfrac{8}{x}$ for $-8 \leqslant x \leqslant -1$.
\newline

(d) Write down the equation of each line of symmetry of the graph.
\newline

{...} and ][]

tex:

 triangle $A$ onto triangle $C$.
\newline

(b) On the grid, draw the image of

(i)  triangle $A$ after a translation by the vector $\begin{pmatrix} -5 \\ 4 \end{pmatrix}$,

 triangle $A$ after a reflection in the line $x = -4.5$.
\newline

(c) The diagram also shows an angle $b$ in triangle $B$.
\newline

Use trigonometry to show that angle $b$ is 63.4°, correct to 1 decimal place.
\newline

(d) 
Two new triangles, $D$ and $E$, are made from triangle $B$, as shown in the diagram.
\newline

Are all three triangles similar?\\
Give a reason for your answer.
\newline

 because
